<!-- Generated by doc-superpowers | 2026-04-05 | commit: 696ba07 -->

# Conventions

## Skill Structure

Follows [obra/superpowers](https://github.com/obra/superpowers) skill conventions:

- **SKILL.md** as the main skill file with YAML frontmatter
- Frontmatter fields: `name`, `description`
- `description` starts with "Use when..." and describes triggering conditions only (never summarizes workflow)
- Supporting files in subdirectories (`references/`) only when content exceeds ~100 lines

## Naming Conventions

| Element | Convention | Example |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| Skill name | lowercase-with-hyphens | `doc-superpowers` |
| Doc files | lowercase-with-hyphens.md | `getting-started.md` |
| Diagram files | lowercase-with-hyphens.png | `c4-context.png` |
| Actions | lowercase single word or hyphenated compound | `init`, `audit`, `sync`, `release`, `review-pr`, `spec-generate` |
| Sections in SKILL.md | Numbered (0-4) with descriptive titles | `## 1. Action Routing` |
| Severity levels | P0-P3 with descriptive label | `P1 Stale` |
| Spec files | `SPEC-{CAT}-NNN-{slug}.md` | `SPEC-API-001-auth-endpoints.md` |
| ADR files | `ADR-NNN-{slug}.md` | `ADR-001-use-content-hashing.md` |

## Versioning

- **Scheme**: Semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH)
- **Changelog**: `RELEASE-NOTES.md` with date, features, fixes per version (see there for current version)
- **Git tags**: `v1.0.0` format

### When to bump

| Change | Version Bump |
|--------|-------------|
| New action added | MINOR |
| New doc template type | MINOR |
| Breaking change to action behavior | MAJOR |
| Bug fix in existing action logic | PATCH |
| Documentation updates only | PATCH |
| New Mermaid diagram type support | MINOR |
| New hook tier added | MINOR |
| Hook behavior change (non-breaking) | PATCH |

### Version Tooling

Version consistency is managed via `doc-tools.sh`:

- **`bump-version VERSION`** — updates the version string in all 7 manifest files simultaneously
- **`check-version`** — verifies all manifest files contain the same version

Manifest files managed: `package.json`, `claude-code.json`, `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`, `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`, `.cursor-plugin/plugin.json`, `gemini-extension.json`, `RELEASE-NOTES.md`.

**After bumping**, also check for stale version references in INSTALL.md files (`.codex/INSTALL.md`, `.opencode/INSTALL.md`) — these contain version-pinned install examples that `bump-version` does not update automatically.

## Git Conventions

### Branches

- `main` — stable releases
- `feature/<name>` — new features
- `fix/<name>` — bug fixes

### Commits

Conventional commits format:

```
feat: add support for <new capability>
fix: correct <specific issue>
docs: update <document> for <reason>
chore: <maintenance task>
```

### Pull Requests

- Keep PRs focused on a single change
- Test skill changes by running `/doc-superpowers init` on a sample project before merging
- Include before/after examples for behavior changes

## Documentation Conventions

### Freshness Markers

Every generated doc file starts with:

```html
<!-- Generated by doc-superpowers | YYYY-MM-DD | commit: SHORT_HASH -->
```

Updated only when the file is regenerated; manually-edited docs keep their markers.

### Template System

- All doc templates live in `references/doc-spec.md`
- Templates use Markdown with placeholder comments (`<!-- description -->`)
- Mermaid diagram syntax is specified per diagram type
- Diagram filenames follow the naming table in doc-spec.md

### Audit Report Format

- Findings sorted by severity: P0 Critical > P1 Stale > P2 Incomplete > P3 Style
- Every finding requires evidence (exact doc quote vs exact code quote)
- No "looks stale" without specific discrepancies
- Includes RELEASE-NOTES.md currency findings (unreleased commits since last version entry)
- See `references/output-templates.md` for the full report format and plan template

### CLAUDE.md Cross-Cutting Sync

Every write action (`init`, `update`, `sync`, `release`, `spec-generate`) must sync CLAUDE.md to reflect the current project state after changes are applied. This includes the Directory Structure tree, Key Files table, Commands section, and any scope-specific entries. See `references/doc-spec.md` for CLAUDE.md update rules.

### README.md Cross-Cutting Sync

Every write action (`init`, `update`, `sync`, `release`, `spec-generate`) must sync README.md to reflect the current project state after changes are applied. This includes the feature list, action list, and usage examples. See `references/doc-spec.md` for README.md update rules. Skip if no README.md exists.

### Archive Conventions

Superseded and deleted docs are moved to `docs/archive/{type}/`:

| Archive Directory | Contents |
|-------------------|----------|
| `docs/archive/adr/` | Superseded ADR files |
| `docs/archive/specs/` | Superseded spec files |
| `docs/archive/plans/` | Completed or superseded plan files |
| `docs/archive/architecture/` | Superseded architecture docs |

Archived docs retain their original filenames. Their `.doc-index.json` entries are updated to `status: "deprecated"`.

## Project-Specific Patterns

### Discovery-First

Every action runs the discovery phase before doing work (except `hooks` and `release`). `hooks` is a scaffolding command that routes directly to the installer; `release` parses RELEASE-NOTES.md and commits directly. This is non-negotiable — generic fallbacks are always less precise than detected tooling.

### Parallel Agent Dispatch

- One agent per documentation scope (not one agent for all docs)
- Each agent gets fresh context with scope-specific focus instructions
- Results merged and deduplicated after collection

### Graceful Degradation

Missing tools trigger fallbacks, never failures:

| Missing | Fallback |
|---------|----------|
| Mermaid MCP | Inline Mermaid source |
| Doc-index (first run) | `check-freshness` reports missing — run `init` to build |
| `jq` | `doc-tools.sh` exits with install instructions |
| User-provided scripts | Bundled tooling handles core operations; optional scripts supplement |

### Evidence-Based Verification

Agent reports without specific evidence (exact quotes from both doc and code) are rejected and re-dispatched. This prevents false confidence from surface-level checks.

## Doc Naming Conventions

Generated documentation follows strict naming conventions for consistency and machine-readability.

### File Names

| Type | Pattern | Example |
|------|---------|---------|
| Specs | `SPEC-{CAT}-NNN-{slug}.md` | `SPEC-API-001-auth-endpoints.md` |
| ADRs | `ADR-NNN-{slug}.md` | `ADR-001-use-content-hashing.md` |
| Architecture docs | `kebab-case.md` | `system-overview.md` |
| Workflow docs | `kebab-case.md` | `ci-cd-pipeline.md` |
| Guide docs | `kebab-case.md` | `getting-started.md` |
| Diagrams | `kebab-case.{png,svg}` | `c4-context.png` |

### Directory Structure

| Directory | Purpose | Diagram Location |
|-----------|---------|-----------------|
| `docs/architecture/` | System overview, component docs | `docs/architecture/diagrams/` |
| `docs/specs/` | Spec index, template, individual specs | n/a |
| `docs/adr/` | ADR log, template, individual ADRs | n/a |
| `docs/workflows/` | Process flows, CI/CD | `docs/workflows/diagrams/` |
| `docs/workflows/agentic/` | Agentic workflow docs | `docs/workflows/diagrams/` |
| `docs/guides/` | Getting started, onboarding | n/a |
| `docs/plans/` | Implementation plans | n/a |
| `docs/archive/` | Superseded/deleted docs by type | n/a |

### Diagram Co-location

Diagrams live in `diagrams/` subdirectories within their parent section, not in a global `docs/diagrams/` directory:
- `docs/architecture/diagrams/c4-context.png` (not `docs/diagrams/c4-context.png`)
- `docs/workflows/diagrams/sequence-audit.png` (not `docs/diagrams/sequence-audit.png`)

## Hooks Conventions

### Tier Options

Hooks are organized into three tiers, each independently installable:

| Tier | Flag | Scripts |
|------|------|---------|
| Git | `--git` | pre-commit, post-merge, post-checkout, prepare-commit-msg, pre-push |
| Claude Code | `--claude` | pre-commit-gate.sh, post-commit-sync.sh, session-summary.sh |
| CI/CD | `--ci` | doc-freshness-pr.yml, doc-freshness-schedule.yml, doc-index-update.yml, doc-audit-update.yml, doc-review-pr.yml, doc-release.yml, doc-spec-verify.yml |

Use `--all` to install all tiers at once.

### Installation Rules

- Hook entries **must always be installed via the installer script** (`scripts/hooks/install.sh`), never manually edited into settings files or hook directories. Manual entries will contain unresolved `__DOC_TOOLS_PATH__` placeholders and will not function correctly.

### Git Hooks Directory Resolution

The installer resolves the git hooks directory using a priority chain:

| Priority | Source | Example |
|----------|--------|---------|
| 1 (highest) | `core.hooksPath` git config | Custom path configured via `git config core.hooksPath <dir>` |
| 2 | `.githooks/` directory | Repo-local convention directory (if it exists) |
| 3 (default) | `.git/hooks/` | Standard git hooks directory |

### Auto-Integration for Existing Git Hooks

When a target hook file already exists and was not installed by doc-superpowers, the installer **auto-integrates** rather than overwriting:

- Copies hook to a local `.doc-superpowers-{name}` file with begin/end markers (`# doc-superpowers:begin`/`# doc-superpowers:end`), uses `dirname $0` for portability
- If the existing hook has a trailing `exit 0`, the source block is inserted before it
- Otherwise, the block is appended to the end of the file
- Uninstall cleanly removes only the integrated lines

### Claude Tier Script Copying

The Claude tier **copies** hook scripts to `.claude/hooks/doc-superpowers/` with placeholder substitution, rather than referencing source scripts by absolute path:

- Scripts are copied with `__DOC_TOOLS_PATH__` and `__INSTALL_DATE__` resolved
- Hooks are registered in `.claude/settings.local.json` using **relative paths** (e.g., `.claude/hooks/doc-superpowers/pre-commit-gate.sh`)
- Existing settings are deep-merged: other hook entries are preserved, and doc-superpowers entries are deduplicated
- Uninstall removes both the settings entries and the copied scripts directory

### CI-Specific Flags

| Flag | Default | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| `--base-branch NAME` | `main` | Target branch for PR checks |
| `--cron EXPR` | `0 9 * * 1` | Schedule for weekly freshness audit |
| `--ci-strict` | off | Fail PR check on stale docs (exit non-zero) |

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Effect |
|----------|--------|
| `DOC_SUPERPOWERS_STRICT=1` | Pre-commit blocks instead of warns |
| `DOC_SUPERPOWERS_QUIET=1` | Suppress hook output while still enforcing |
| `DOC_SUPERPOWERS_SKIP=1` | Bypass all hooks temporarily |

## Spec Lifecycle Conventions

### Status Transitions

Spec files use a `Status` field that follows a linear progression:

| Status | Meaning | Set By |
|--------|---------|--------|
| `Draft` | Spec generated, not yet implemented | `spec-generate` |
| `In Review` | Implementation started, spec under active verification | `spec-inject --phase=execute` |
| `Implemented` | Verification passed, spec matches code | `spec-verify --mode=post-execute` |

Transitions: `Draft` → `In Review` (first implementation chunk) → `Implemented` (verification passes).

### Supersession Conventions

When a new design doc replaces an existing spec's scope:

1. New spec is created with `Supersedes: <path-to-old>` in metadata
2. Old spec's `Superseded by: <path-to-new>` field is updated
3. Old spec is moved to `docs/archive/specs/`
4. Old spec's `.doc-index.json` entry is set to `status: "deprecated"`

The `replaces` and `superseded_by` fields in `.doc-index.json` track the chain:

| Field | Value | Meaning |
|-------|-------|---------|
| `replaces` | path or null | Doc path this entry supersedes |
| `superseded_by` | path or null | Doc path that supersedes this entry |

### Spec Numbering

Specs use sequential numbering within their category: `SPEC-{CAT}-NNN-{slug}.md`. If extending an existing category (not superseding), use the next sequential number (e.g., `SPEC-AUTH-002` if `SPEC-AUTH-001` exists).

## Doc-Index Schema Conventions

The `docs/.doc-index.json` file is the machine-readable freshness index. It follows these conventions:

### Read/Write Separation

- **`build-index`** creates the index from scratch (filesystem scan)
- **`update-index`** modifies individual entries (targeted updates)
- **`check-freshness`** is read-only (reports staleness without modifying)
- **`status`** is read-only (summarizes current state)

### Status Transitions

| From | To | Trigger |
|------|----|---------|
| (new) | `current` | `build-index` creates entry with matching hashes |
| `current` | `stale` | `check-freshness` detects hash mismatch or code change |
| `stale` | `current` | `update-index` after doc is updated |
| `current` | `deprecated` | Human-set only (edit index directly), or spec supersession via `spec-generate` |
| `deprecated` | — | Terminal state for automated tools |

`deprecated` is a terminal state for automated tools.

### Entry Schema

Each entry in the index (keyed by relative doc path) contains:
- `content_hash` — `sha256:<hex>` hash of doc file content
- `code_refs` — list of directories/files this doc covers
- `code_commit` — latest git commit SHA touching any `code_refs` at index build time
- `doc_type` — template type: `architecture`, `workflows`, `api-contracts`, `spec`, `adr`, etc.
- `status` — one of `current`, `stale`, `deprecated`
- `replaces` — path to doc this one supersedes (null if none)
- `superseded_by` — path to doc that supersedes this one (null if none)
- `last_verified` — ISO 8601 timestamp of last freshness check confirmation
